input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="My App">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page as a global route so you can
        // navigate to it from anywhere with a URI.
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (from either products list):

```csharp
// Pass the product ID (or any data) as a query parameter
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
    $"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive it in `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty]`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
    // ...
}
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"` enables the hamburger menu.
- Each `FlyoutItem` is one row in the flyout. Nesting two `<Tab>` elements inside the Products item gives you the bottom tab bar on that section.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` makes `ProductDetailsPage` reachable via URI-based navigation without placing it in the visual hierarchy.

1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page accessible from flyout
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage
